The rapper’s ensemble “felt effortless but considered, merging heritage codes with a modern edge,” she says.
Gracie Abrams
Gillette cited Gracie Abrams as one more star who nailed the Met Gala’s theme this year.
The “That’s So True” singer “absolutely glowed” in her Chanel gown, which was inspired by by a Gustav Klimt painting, says Gillette.
“With clear reference to the famous painter, it hit the fashion as art theme out of the park in the most beautiful way,” she adds.

title: See the Trailer for Season 2 of OWN’s ‘Heart & Hustle: Houston’
summary: Watch the trailer for Season 2 of of OWN’s "Heart & Hustle: Houston" and find out when the new season premieres. Plus, catch up with the cast.
story: “It’s all about sisterhood,” “Heart & Hustle: Houston” star Chloe Cook says in a trailer for the new season. But based on the footage, some friendships might be broken beyond repair.

The OWN reality television series, which follows a group of Black women looking for love and striving for success in Houston, will soon return for Season 2. This season, all the main cast members are back — Cook, La’Torria Lemon, LeBrina Jackson, Muneera Page, Roe Grady-Pichardo and Alaina Saulsberry — to face one other after the fallouts from Season 1.
TODAY.com is exclusively revealing the trailer for Season 2, which shows some of the ladies breaking off into separate groups as they struggle to rebuild their friendships.

In the teaser, the ladies are initially seen celebrating each other and exploring their romantic connections. Next, the clip takes a turn. “Life’s good, but behind the scenes…” Cook begins before the tone changes.

Saulsberry opens up about struggling to keep her family together. Elsewhere, Grady-Pichardo sheds tears as she worries about her children feeling like she is keeping them from their father. An emotional Lemon says, “I feel like my mom gave up. And not only did she give up on everything, she gave up on me.”
Page and Cook confront the men in their lives, with Cook wondering if she was cheated on.
However, the trailer hints that not everything is as it seems. “There are two sides to every story,” Jackson notes.
Some of the women are also not on speaking terms and one is called out for being a “mean girl.”

All the drama will unfold when Season 2 of “Heart & Hustle: Houston” premieres on OWN Saturday, May 30, at 9 p.m. ET/PT. New episodes will then air regularly at 8 p.m. ET/PT starting Saturday, June 20.
Watch the full clip and then get a refresher on everything you need to know about the cast, below.
‘Heart & Hustle: Houston’ Season 2 Cast
The six main cast members of “Heart & Hustle: Houston” are all reuniting for Season 2. Here’s what you need to remember about each member of the friend group and what to expect this season.

Cook is hoping to take the next step in her relationship with Jordan, but trust issues pop up before he can propose. Meanwhile, Cook’s family is skeptical about the couple’s 17-year age gap. As for her career, Cook leans into her passion for event planning and tries to rebuild her business.
Lemon is busy running her award-winning public relations firm and is also looking to expand her philanthropic impact. Privately, she is grieving the loss of her father and is looking to heal.

Jackson wants to further her progress in the wellness space as the owner of an elite spa. It seems she could be the “mean girl” mentioned in the trailer as her relationship with the other women becomes fractured.
Page is managing being a mom, wife and career woman. She and her husband, Norm, are struggling with their emotional connection and she hopes to repair their relationship.
Grady-Pichardo is an innovator and restaurateur that is involved in the Houston nightlife scene. Outside of work, she is a single mother who is co-parenting with her ex, Jayvon.
Saulsberry, the CEO of a makeup company, is comfortable in her work life after building a beauty empire. But outside of work, she and her husband, Josh, are separated and heading toward a divorce.

title: Whitney Leavitt Speaks Out About ‘SLOMW’ Exit and When She’s Leaving
summary: Whitney Leavitt is confirming she will exit “The Secret Lives of Mormon Lives” and when she plans to leave the Hulu reality TV series
story: Whitney Leavitt is confirming she will exit “The Secret Lives of Mormon Lives” and when she plans to leave the Hulu reality TV series.
The 32-year-old actor said in a May 5 selfie video on Instagram that the upcoming fifth season will be her last.

Leavitt’s update comes after she disclosed her exit from the show during her final performance in “Chicago” on Broadway on May 3, according to Deadline. Leavitt had been playing Roxie Hart since her debut in February.

“It’s honestly so crazy to me looking back on this journey because I had been trying to get into theater and film way before ‘Secret Lives’ even came into my life,” Leavitt said in her Instagram video.
She explained that “Secret Lives” fell into her life “organically.”
“It’s definitely not the path that I had envisioned in my mind to get to where I am today, but I wouldn’t change a thing,” she added.

The former MomTok influencer, who has been a cast member on the Hulu series since it premiered in September 2024, went on to say she “experienced so much” with the other women on the show and “learned so much” about herself.

“We have been through so much together, even more than what you’ve seen,” said Leavitt. “We’ve had really big highs, really big lows and no matter what happens with our relationships like that is something that will always be a part of our life, will always be a part of my life, and I wouldn’t change a thing.”
Leavitt concluded by saying she was leaving the reality series “with gratitude.”
“I feel content,” she said. “I feel like this is a chapter that’s closing in my life and honestly, I believe that’s how it was always meant to be.

“I’m so grateful for the ‘Secret Lives of Mormon Wives,’” she added. “It’s got me where I am today. It’s given me the opportunities that you all have seen but I’m ready. I’m ready for the next chapter and I can not wait to share with you guys what’s next.”

Leavitt’s starring role in “Chicago” proved to be wildly successful for the musical. She extended her role in March because of what the show’s producers called “an overwhelming demand” for tickets, People reported at the time. With Leavitt in as the show’s lead, “Chicago” broke records with the highest weekly ticket sales in the show’s 29-year history, according to People.

title: What’s It Like Inside the Met Gala? These Photos Give a Glimpse Behind the Scenes
summary: The Met Gala has long been considered fashion’s biggest night, and the 2026 event certainly lived up to those expectations
story: The Met Gala has long been considered fashion’s biggest night, and the 2026 event certainly lived up to those expectations.
On May 4, Hollywood A-listers stepped out in style to attend the annual charity fundraiser and fashion exhibition at New York’s Metropolitan Museum of Art.
Revisit the all top moments from the 2026 Met Gala

This year’s theme, “Costume Art,” featured a dress code described as “fashion is art.” Watching attendees walk the red carpet is always the highlight of the night for fashion lovers, especially since there’s a no-phones policy inside the event, per The Hollywood Reporter.
But just because celebrities can’t share a look inside, that doesn’t mean there aren’t photographers on hand.

Lucky for us, the professionals captured behind-the-scenes snapshots that revealed a few outfit changes and some fun celebrity pairings. Below, we’re sharing a few of the standout photos of the night.
Sabrina Carpenter's Outfit Changes
On the red carpet, Sabrina Carpenter sported a Dior gown that was made of cascading strips of film and accessorized with a jeweled headpiece.
Inside the event, the singer changed into a vibrant dress with a crisscross neckline and prints of Andy Warhol’s pop art of Marilyn Monroe.

While performing with Stevie Nicks, Carpenter slipped into yet another ensemble: a gold spaghetti strap mini dress with shimmery tassels. Meanwhile, Nicks also looked gorgeous in an embellished black gown.
Kendall Jenner's Wings
Kendall Jenner channeled her inner Greek goddess at the 2026 Met Gala in a Zac Posen gown that was inspired by “Winged Victory of Samothrace,” a statue in the Louvre museum in Paris.
While speaking with E! News’ La La Anthony, Posen said Jenner’s ensemble may include a “surprise” that would make its debut inside the event.
Photographers captured a photo of the stunning surprise: a set of wings that helped her channel the statue even more.
The Fashionable Co-Chairs
Alongside Anna Wintour, the 2026 Met Gala co-chairs included Nicole Kidman, Beyoncé and Venus Williams.
On the red carpet, Kidman opted for a red sequined Chanel gown as she posed for photos alongside daughter Sunday Rose, 17.
Meanwhile, Beyoncé walked the red carpet in an embellished Olivier Rousteing ensemble as she was accompanied by her 14-year-old daughter, Blue Ivy Carter, who made her Met Gala debut.
Williams also understood the assignment and showed up in a Swarovski crystal mesh gown, which she paired with a Wimbledon plate-inspired necklace.

Inside the event, two of the co-chairs changed their outfits. Kidman slipped into a red and cream gown with feathers and embellishments. Beyoncé brought the drama in a black long sleeve gown with sequins for days, a wide train and a veil she wore over her face.
Sza, Doechii and Hailey Bieber

Inside the event, attendees have the opportunity to catch up with old friends and mingle with some new faces. Photographers caught Sza, Doechii and Hailey Bieber together inside and snapped this stunning shot.

Per Vogue, Bieber was sporting a custom Saint Laurent dress with a 24-karat gold sculpted bodice. Doechii rocked a burgundy Marc Jacobs wrap dress and Sza opted for a yellow Bode dress with a larger-than-life headpiece.
Rihanna, Ciara and Katy Perry
One photographer captured this photo of three female powerhouse singers chatting inside the event.
Rihanna, who spoke with TODAY's Sheinelle Jones on the red carpet, wore a sculptural gold and silver metallic look from Maison Margiela FW25 Couture.

On the red carpet, Perry kept her identity under wraps at first while wearing a mesh and silver mask with her white off-the-shoulder Stella McCartney gown. Inside the event, she took the mask off to pose for a photo.
Ciara also dressed to impress in an Egyptian-inspired gold gown and headpiece.
The ‘Heated Rivalry’ Guys
"Heated Rivalry" stars Connor Storrie and Hudson William appeared to have a blast inside the event. In one photo, Storrie was seen dancing alongside Tate McRae and Blackpink singer Rosé.
Williams also mingled with a member of Blackpink, Lisa, at the event and showed off his keen fashion sense in a custom Balenciaga look.

title: Tina Knowles Says Beyonce’s Full Circle Moment at the Met Gala ‘Brought Tears to My Eyes’
summary: Beyoncé's mother, Tina Knowles, described how she felt watching her daughter return to the Met Gala, with granddaughter, Blue Ivy.
story: Beyoncé attended the Met Gala for the first time in a decade this year. It was an appearance that felt like déjà vu to her mother.

The “Halo” singer served as co-chair for the May 4 gala, which marked the first time she turned out for the event in 10 years. She was joined this year by husband Jay-Z and their daughter, Blue Ivy, 14.
“Oh, my God, I got really emotional. Shed a few tears,” Beyoncé’s mother, Tina Knowles, said when she appeared May 5 on the 3rd hour of TODAY.
Knowles said it was a full circle moment.

“It was kind of reminiscent, because Beyoncé started, you know, her first red carpet at 15, and Blue is 14. And so just seeing her out there looking confident and beautiful, it just brought tears to my eyes.”
Al Roker noted that Beyoncé and Blue “were like looking at both versions of the same (person) at the same time.”
“It’s like déjà vu,” Knowles added.
Knowles was also wowed by Sheinelle Jones dressing up as her daughter for TODAY’s Halloween spectacular last year.
“She was amazing,” Knowles said. “I posted it on my Instagram, and I couldn’t wait to show Beyoncé. She was so excited.”

Beyonce, who got a shoutout from Sheinelle at the Met Gala, has also spurred rumors that act three of her planned trilogy is coming any day now, even as TODAY.com has learned a release following 2023’s “Renaissance” and 2024’s “Cowboy Carter” is not imminent.
Don’t ask her mother, though, when to expect it.
“You know, I’ve said it before. I could tell you, but I’d have to kill you,” she joked.

title: Timothée Chalamet Was a No-Show at The Met Gala. Where Was He?
summary: The "Marty Supreme" star had to choose between the iconic fashion event and his beloved New York Knicks on May 4.
story: Timothée Chalamet had a choice to make on May 4: the fashion event of the year or his beloved New York Knicks.

The "Marty Supreme" star, who co-chaired the 2021 Met Gala and has been a regular at the iconic event, was faced with a dilemma when the Knicks eliminated the Atlanta Hawks in the first round of the NBA playoffs.
The first game of the Knicks' Eastern Conference semifinal series against the Philadelphia 76ers fell on the same day as the 2026 Met Gala, with both of them being held in his native New York City.

Chalamet, 30, ended up skipping the "Costume Art" theme at the Metropolitan Museum of Art for the blue-and-orange theme at a raucous, sold-out Madison Square Garden. He was rewarded for his decision when the Knicks obliterated the Sixers 137-98 to take a 1-0 lead in the series.
He posted a shot on his Instagram story of him resting his sneakers on the floor at MSG.

The actor probably didn't plan on facing the conflict with the Met Gala because the Sixers were an underdog to the Boston Celtics in the first round. Had the Celtics won, Monday night's game against the Knicks would have been in Boston. Instead, the Sixers stunned the Celtics in Game 7 of their first-round series, giving the Knicks homecourt advantage in the semifinals and making the first game at MSG.

This is the second straight year the Knicks have prompted Chalamet to skip the Met Gala. He was not there in 2025, either, and posted a shot on his Instagram story of him watching the Knicks' playoff game on an iPad at the time of the event.
Chalamet spoke with former Knicks star Carmelo Anthony on Anthony's 7PM in Brooklyn podcast in December 2025 about skipping the Met Gala that year.
"I had to be locked in, man," he said. "That Knicks' run last year was the most fun — I think that was the most fun period of my life, those four months. That was just the Garden being electric."
Chalamet's longtime girlfriend, Kylie Jenner, made sure the couple was represented at the 2026 Met Gala by stunning in a sleeveless Schiaparelli gown and what appeared to be bleached eyebrows.

Jenner, 28, has been a fixture at the Met Gala since her first appearance at the annual event in 2016. She was flying solo as Chalamet sat courtside at MSG to cheer on the Knicks' third straight playoff victory by 25 or more points.

Chalamet grew up as a diehard Knicks fan and has been a courtside fixture at MSG alongside regulars like Spike Lee, Tracy Morgan, Ben Stiller and Susie Essman. Lee has also been a former attendee at the Met Gala, but he was in attendance with the Knicks' faithful at MSG on Monday night.

title: Kellie Pickler Returns to ‘American Idol’ With 2006 Alums, 3 Years After Husband’s Death
summary: “American Idol” star Kellie Pickler returned to the show to perform with Hanna Harper on an episode celebrating the 30th anniversary of the show's fifth season.
story: “American Idol” welcomed back one of its own on the May 4 episode of the ABC stalwart.
Kellie Pickler, who finished in sixth place during the show’s fifth season in 2006, returned to the singing competition, as part of “Idol’s” celebration of that season.
Pickler performed with current semifinalist Hannah Harper, as the pair teamed up to sing Martina McBride’s “A Broken Wing.”
“I’m an antique ‘Idol’ dustin’ off,” the “Best Days of Your Life” singer said after the performance, according to E! Online.

“Being able to share the stage with you has been such a blessing,” she added, to Harper, who managed to avoid elimination, while advancing to the May 11 finale, along with Keyla Richardson and Jordan McCullough.

Pickler was joined on “Idol” by fellow finalists from the 2006 season, including Elliott Yamin, Paris Bennett, Bucky Covington and Taylor Hicks, who won that season. Each of them performed a duet with one of the five remaining hopefuls, while original “American Idol” judges Paula Abdul and Randy Jackson also came back to serve as guest judges.

Pickler’s “Idol” appearance marked her first time on the show since her husband, Kyle Jacobs, died in 2023 at the age of 49. Pickler has kept a low profile since then, last performing at a Patsy Cline tribute in 2024.
“One of the most beautiful lessons my husband taught me was in a moment of a crisis, 'if you don’t know what to do, do nothing, just be still,’” Pickler told People in August 2023.
“I have chosen to heed his advice.”

title: Blue Ivy Carter, 14, Becomes One of the Youngest Met Gala Attendees — See the Best Pics
summary: Blue Ivy Carter, daughter of Jay-Z and Beyoncé, attended the Met Gala 2026 in Balenciaga. She posed with her parents and alone.
story: Blue Ivy Carter was ready for her Met Gala moment.
The 14-year-old daughter of Beyoncé and Jay-Z made her debut at the iconic fashion event on May 4, dazzling in Balenciaga while appearing alongside her parents.

Her first Met Gala coincided with Beyoncé's return to the event for the first time in 10 years, as the "Texas Hold 'Em" singer was one of four co-chairs of the annual affair at the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York City.
Blue Ivy wore a billowing white gown with a long train that had a matching jacket, all of which was designed by Balenciaga.

She also had on sunglasses and silver stiletto heels as she posed alongside Jay-Z, who looked dapper in a black suit by Louis Vuitton, and Beyoncé, who stunned in a diamond-encrusted skeletal gown that was custom-made by Olivier Rousteing.

Blue Ivy's appearance was surprising in that she is the rare minor to attend the event. Organizers told The Hollywood Reporter in 2018 that the Met Gala "is not an appropriate event for people under 18," but exceptions are made for minors accompanied by their parents.
Blue Ivy was 4 years old the last time Beyoncé attended the Met Gala before Monday night, so her mother soaked in the special moment.
“It feels surreal because my daughter’s here,” Beyoncé told Vogue during a livestream of the event. “She looks so beautiful — it’s incredible to be able to share it with her.”
Blue Ivy looked like a seasoned veteran as she posed solo and with her parents.
“She was ready!" Beyoncé said on the livestream. "She is ready."

The teen is no stranger to sharing the spotlight with her mother, as she joined Beyoncé onstage during her "Cowboy Carter Tour" in 2024 and posed with her on the red carpet in 2023 at the London premiere of her "Renaissance" concert film.
She also made her big screen debut as the voice of Kiara in the 2024 animated movie "Mufasa: The Lion King."
Blue Ivy also has become known for her style, which she showed off in December while sitting courtside with Jay-Z at a Los Angeles Lakers game.

title: On the Met Gala Carpet, Rihanna Gave Sheinelle an Inspirational Talk She’ll Never Forget
summary: Rihanna shared words of encouragement for Sheinelle Jones on the red carpet of the Met Gala.
story: Sheinelle Jones got a seal of approval from one of the world’s biggest pop stars at the Met Gala on May 4.

While reporting from the red carpet in New York City for TODAY on fashion’s biggest night of the year, Sheinelle received some serious words of encouragement from Rihanna, who attended with her longtime partner, A$AP Rocky.
Revisit all the best moments from the 2026 Met Gala
“So, what was your day like today?” the singer asked.
“Today was, honestly — hoping that I could meet the moment and catch somebody’s eye contact,” Sheinelle answered.

Rihanna, who wore a sculpted gold and silver gown by Maison Margiela FW25 Couture with crystals and beads wrapped in a gold floor-length circular shawl, then replied with a strong dose of affirmation that touched on Sheinelle’s career, as well as her sense of style.

“Well, you deserve to be poured into, as well. And you’re beautiful. You’re a Black journalist. And I respect you. I love you for this. And, by the way, your outfit is f------ fire,” she told Sheinelle.
“I love you, Rihanna,” Sheinelle replied.

The theme for this year’s Met Gala was “Fashion Is Art,” which went hand in hand with the Metropolitan Museum of Art’s spring exhibition of “Costume Art.” This year’s gala once again featured a litany of A-list stars, from Anna Wintour and Dwayne Johnson to Amanda Seyfried and Beyoncé, who was making her first appearance at the event in a decade, while co-chairing this year’s festivities.

Sheinelle was determined to see the Grammy-winning star, who wore a custom Olivier Rousteing gown covered in a silver crystals and was accompanied by husband Jay-Z and their 14-year-old daughter, Blue Ivy.
“Beyoncé, can I just tell you that we celebrate you?” Sheinelle shouted to her as the “Texas Hold ‘Em” singer posed for photos. “We celebrate you.”
It appeared as if Beyoncé mouthed something to Sheinelle, which delighted her.
“All I wanted was eye contact and a loving gaze — I’m happy,” Sheinelle said.

title: Mariska Hargitay Reveals New Bob Haircut Ahead of Broadway Debut — See the Pics
summary: Mariska Hargitay just ditched her long locks for something a bit breezier.
story: Mariska Hargitay just ditched her long locks for something a bit breezier.
The "Law & Order: Special Victims Unit" star debuted a brand new bob haircut in a series of photos posted by her hairstylist, Adir Abergel.
The fresh cut comes just in time for the actor’s Broadway debut in the solo show “Every Brilliant Thing” this month. Hargitay will be taking over for Daniel Radcliffe on May 26.

Abergel hinted that Hargitay's upcoming stint on Broadway inspired the look in his post's caption, writing, "NEW CUT FOR THE ONE AND ONLY @therealmariskahargitay BROADWAY DEBUT IN @brilliantbway ❤️❤️❤️❤️."
Hargitay also commented on the post, writing, "Switching gears."
Abergel highlighted his handiwork in the photo series, which showed Hargitay's chin-length, wavy bob in all its glory.
The actor's fans reacted to her new look in the comments section.
"It should be criminal to look this good!" the official Instagram page for “Law & Order” wrote.
One fan commented, "OMG!!! This is incredible on her 👏👏👏."
Another wrote: "OBSESSED with the bob!!! It is so so good. How rude of Mariska to be able to pull off basically anything and look absolutely fabulous."

Abergel just shared photos of Hargitay's new hairstyle, but the actor gave fans a first look at the haircut on April 26 in a video promoting her Broadway debut. In the short clip, she danced at a photoshoot with confetti covering the floor.

In March, Playbill shared details about Hargitay's upcoming role in "Every Brilliant Thing," a solo play that first debuted at Edinburgh Festival Fringe. In the play, the protagonist "faces a distant father, a suicidal mother, and his own inexplicable deep sadness."

"I read 'Every Brilliant Thing' and cried, rejoiced, laughed, cried some more, and loved it so much,” Hargitay said in a statement to Playbill. "I’m always drawn to themes of healing and renewal, especially when the journey is rendered in all its complexity. It feels like an extraordinary gift to make my Broadway debut, the fulfillment of a lifelong dream, with a play that affirms life so emphatically."
Hargitay's first performance will take place on May 26. The show will run through June 28.

title: Dolly Parton Reveals She Is Canceling Vegas Residency Due to Health Issues: 'A Little Bad News'
summary: Dolly Parton is sharing an encouraging update on her ongoing health struggles, but it came with "a little bad news" too, she said: She is canceling her upcoming Las Vegas residency."The good news is: I'm responding really well to meds and treatments and I'm improving every day," she said in a video posted to her Instagram on May 4."Now the bad news is: It’s going to take me a little while before I'm up to stage performance level because some of the meds and treatments make me a little bit 'swi
story: Dolly Parton is sharing an encouraging update on her ongoing health struggles, but it came with "a little bad news" too, she said: She is canceling her upcoming Las Vegas residency.
"The good news is: I'm responding really well to meds and treatments and I'm improving every day," she said in a video posted to her Instagram on May 4.

"Now the bad news is: It’s going to take me a little while before I'm up to stage performance level because some of the meds and treatments make me a little bit 'swimmy' headed, as my grandma used to say," Parton said in the video.
The 80-year-old went on to compare herself to “an old classic car that once restored can be better than ever.”

“But when they raised the hood on this old antique, they realized that I need to rebuild my engine and that my transmission is slipping, my oil pan is leaking and my muffler’s busted and my shocks and pistons need to be replaced,” she said.
More specifically, Parton said she has "always had problems" with kidney stones.

"Lord, they dig more stones out of me a year than the rock quarry in Rockwood, Tennessee," she joked. "But seriously, my immune system and my digestive system got all out of whack over the past couple three years, and they're working real hard on rebuilding and strengthening those. And hopefully, I'll be up to snuff again soon."
She did not share further details on her health struggles.
Parton said she's trying to keep everything "light and airy" and has been keeping busy working on several professional projects as she navigates her health challenges.
The singer previously had to postpone her Vegas concerts in September 2025 as she focused on her health.
"On a more serious note, I'm truly sorry that I'm going to miss all of you that had tickets to see me in Las Vegas," she said.
During her video message, Parton also thanked her fans for their support after her husband, Carl Dean, died in 2025.
"I will always love him, and I'll always miss him, but you would be surprised at how much your love and concern meant to me during that time," she said.
At the end of her video message, Parton said she has "great doctors" and is "doing really well."
"And they assure me that everything I have is treatable. So I'm going with that," she said.

title: The 2026 Met Gala Guest List: Here Are All the Stars Who Attended
summary: It's the first Monday in May and that means fashion’s biggest night out is officially here
story: It's the first Monday in May and that means fashion’s biggest night out is officially here.
The 2026 Met Gala is expected, per usual, to be a star-studded and A-list affair, with some of the biggest and most impressive names in music, movies, sports, fashion and more in attendance.
Follow live coverage

The guest list was shared on Monday afternoon with the media lining up at the red carpet. This came after a slew of stars were already revealed to be attending as the gala’s co-chairs and committee members for 2026.
This year’s co-chairs Beyoncé, Venus Williams and Nicole Kidman, as well as Anna Wintour, now in her new role as Condé Nast’s chief content officer.
Kidman and Williams attended the 2025 Met Gala, but this will be Beyoncé's first appearance in a decade.

The four chairs will be joined by Anthony Vaccarello and Zoë Kravitz, who will also co-chair the celebration's host committee that includes additional celebrities who will undoubtedly be dressed to the nines in designer wear.

The 2026 Met Gala theme is “Costume Art,” with the dress code described as “fashion is art.” Beyoncé, Kidman, Williams and the rest of the VIP guests are expected to interpret the theme and dress code by expressing “their own relationship to fashion as an embodied artform,” according to Vogue’s guidelines.

At last year's Met Gala, Rihanna surprised guests, fans and Met Gala viewers by revealing her pregnancy, André 3000 literally carried a piano on his back, and Bad Bunny honored his Puerto Rican roots, while Zendaya and Anna Sawai had nearly identical looks.
Expect many jaw-dropping, head-turning, inspirational and, perhaps, shocking looks as the stars make their way up the celebrated stairs at the Metropolitan Museum of Art.
Below, see a list of 2026 Met Gala attendees. This story will be updated with photos as guests arrive.
Met Gala 2026 Guest List

title: Rihanna (Again) Steals the Show at 2026 Met Gala
summary: Rihanna arrived fashionably late to the Met Gala on the arm of longtime love A$AP Rocky
story: Rihanna arrived fashionably late to the Met Gala on the arm of longtime love A$AP Rocky.

Rihanna donned a sculptural gold and silver metallic look from Maison Margiela FW25 Couture, featuring a long-sleeve top embellished in crystals and beads. She was wrapped in a ribbony gold shawl that reached down to the floor and resembled a golden cocoon.
Follow for live updates
She completed her look with metallic curls woven into her hair and large rings and other pieces of eye-catching jewelry from Briony Raymond and Dyne jewelry.
A$AP Rocky wore a pink Chanel tuxedo with black lapels.
The couple have made the annual event a kind of family affair over the years.
Rihanna stole the show on the red carpet of the 2025 Met Gala by revealing she was expecting her third child, Rocki Irish, who was born in September 2025.
Actually, it was A$AP Rocky who broke the pregnancy news just ahead of the "Diamonds" singer's entrance.
“Rihanna’s outfit tonight is ... I don’t know, whatever, just don’t cover her baby hump, you dig?” he said in an interview on the carpet.
Rihanna strolled into the gala with a look that was picture perfect for the night's theme, “Superfine: Tailoring Black Style.”
She wore a cropped jacket with a crossover necktie and a gray corseted top that did cover her baby bump.
Her floor-length skirt resembled a suit jacket, and she topped it all off with a wide-brim black hat.
As she made her way to speak with TODAY’s Al Roker, a fan jokingly called out to ask Rihanna if she’d brought her “umbrella-ella-ella, eh” — a play on her hit 2007 single “Umbrella.”
“I brought the kid!” Rihanna responded.
Rihanna has earned raves for her Met Gala looks over the years.

The singer and Fenty mogul has been a steady presence at the event since making her gala debut in 2007. She wore a white Georges Chakra dress that she paired with black fishnet gloves for her first outing.
In 2009, she wore a Dolce & Gabba tux with puffed sleeves. She topped off the look with slicked back hair.

In 2011, she put her own spin on that year's “Alexander McQueen: Savage Beauty” theme by wearing a curve-hugging lace Stella McCartney gown paired with long red hair done up in a braid over her shoulder.
For 2015's “China: Through the Looking Glass” theme, she wore a regal Guo Pei-designed yellow coat-dress with long train and crown.
In 2017, she wore a wild sculptural floral Commes des Garçons gown for the gala's “Rei Kawakubo: Art Of The In-Between” theme.

In 2018, she took fashion cues from the Vatican for the gala's “Heavenly Bodies: Fashion and the Catholic Imagination” theme. She wore a rhinestone-encrusted minidress with a coat — and, yes, a papal headdress — designed by John Galliano for Maison Margiela.
In 2021, Rihanna wore a Balenciaga overcoat that she paired with  267 carats of Bulgari jewelry.
In 2023, she arrived an hour and a half late to pay homage to Karl Lagerfeld by wearing a custom white hooded Valentino Haute Couture gown adorned with camellia flowers and a long train.
The singer later removed the outer part of her ensemble to reveal a strappy white gown and a baby bump, after revealing her pregnancy during her Super Bowl halftime show.

title: Heidi Klum Went Just as Hard at the Met Gala as She Does for Halloween
summary: Heidi Klum brought her love of Halloween costumes to the 2026 Met Gala
story: Heidi Klum brought her love of Halloween costumes to the 2026 Met Gala.
Klum, who throws one of Hollywood’s most famous Halloween parties every year, got into the spooky spirit to honor the event’s “Fashion is Art” dress code this year.
Follow for live updates

The 52-year-old supermodel and “Project Runway” host arrived at the gala dressed as a marble statue in a custom look created by her longtime Halloween collaborator, Oscar-nominated make-up artist Mike Marino, Klum revealed on Instagram.
The look was inspired by classical sculptures such as “Veiled Vestal” by Raffaele Monti.
Marino “transformed fabric into sculpture, manipulating latex and spandex with extraordinary precision to mirror the stillness, delicacy, and illusion of carved marble,” Klum explained.
Klum appeared to be carved out of marble and then draped with a gauzy white sheet. She wore ancient-looking sandals on her feet, a simple wreath on her head and creepy looking contact lenses.
Klum also expressed her enthusiasm for this year’s gala dress code.
“I love fashion, I love art, and I especially love when the two collide,” she wrote.

Klum’s Met Gala look isn’t too far a cry from her 2025 Halloween costume. The model transformed into Medusa, the terrifying figure from Greek mythology who has snakes instead of hair. So frightening looking was Medusa that anyone who dared look at her turned to stone.
For the costume, Klum wore a prosthetic forked tongue and had fake snakes moving around on her head. Scales covered her face and body and she wore bright green contact lenses.
Klum’s husband Tom Kaulitz walked the red carpet with her dressed as one of Medusa’s victims: a stone figure.
Klum told People magazine in 2025 that she sees creating her costumes each year as a kind of “art.”

“I never take the easy way out,” she said. “People say, ‘Why the heck are you doing this?’ I do it for the art of it, of transformation, of surprising people also. It’s like a life performance art thing in a way because you see so much AI and things are being photographed, repatched, this, that and the other.”
“I always try to come up with something that is giving people an ‘ooh’ and ‘ahh’ and a wow because I want to inspire people to also do something creative,” she shares.

title: Kim Kardashian’s Met Gala 2026 Look Could Be From the Future (But It’s From the Past)
summary: Kim Kardashian appeared to have stepped out of the future when she arrived at the 2026 Met Gala
story: Kim Kardashian appeared to have stepped out of the future when she arrived at the 2026 Met Gala.
The reality star and Skims co-founder, 45, wore a bronze breastplate and an ankle-length open skirt in a daring look she collaborated on with artist Alan Jones.
Follow for live updates

“We used his body plates that he started making in the ’60s, and this is actually a mold from a model from the ‘60s that we made together, and he painted on top of it," Kardashian told E! News' La La Anthony of the pair's collaboration.
Kardashian completed her look, which nods to the event's "Fashion is Art" theme this year, with tousled bond hair and tan heels.
Kardashian made her gala debut alongside then partner Kanye West in 2013 when she was pregnant with the couple's first child, daughter North West.
Kardashian wore a custom floral Givenchy gown by designer Riccardo Tisci that showed off her baby bump.

The following year, the former couple showed up in classic Old Hollywood looks. Kardashian wore a blue strapless Lanvin gown with her hair straight and parted to the side. West wore a black tuxedo with coattails.

Fast forward to Kardashian's edgier look in 2018 when she paid tribute to the night's theme, “Heavenly Bodies: Fashion and the Catholic Imagination," by wearing a gold chainmail Versace dress embellished with crosses.
The following year she wore a custom corseted gown by Thierry Mugler, marking the designer's first new look in 20 years.
Kardashian made headlines again in 2021 when she showed up to the Met Gala in a dramatic black Balenciaga look that covered her from head to toe.

When Kardashian attended the 2022 gala with then-boyfriend Pete Davidson, she wore the iconic dress Marilyn Monroe wore in 1962 when she sang happy birthday to President John F. Kennedy at Madison Square Garden.
Last year, Kardashian paid homage to the gala's “Superfine: Tailoring Black Style” theme by wearing a two-piece leather Chrome Hearts look that she topped off with a leather hat.

title: Bad Bunny Ages 50 Years for 2026 Met Gala
summary: Bad Bunny once again delivered a fresh and unique look for the Met Gala.
story: Bad Bunny gave fans a glimpse of his future self at the 2026 Met Gala.

The fashion-loving rapper showed up to the star-studded event looking many years older. He wore prosthetics to add wrinkles, saggy skin and sun spots to his face and hands while his hair and beard were white.
Follow for live updates
The 32-year-old musician, whose real name is Benito Antonio Martínez Ocasio, cut a sophisticated figure wearing a black tuxedo with a large bow he designed in collaboration with Zara, reported Vogue.

"Part of my life (is) always try to do something different and and this day of the Met is a perfect day to you explore and be creative and express yourself in a different way. So that’s, that’s what I’m doing tonight," Bad Bunny told E! News' La La Anthony on the event's red carpet.
When Anthony asked Bad Bunny how many hours it took to create his look, he cheekily responded, "53 years, exactly, 53 years."
"So it took a little bit, but it worth it. I hope he looked good. Do I look good?" he asked.
Bad Bunny's bold Met Gala look is hardly his first. In the past, he's shown up to the event in gender-bending ensembles that frequently pay tribute to his Puerto Rico homeland.
For his Met Gala debut in 2022, Bad Bunny gave the night's gilded glamour dress code a Puerto Rican twist.
“I researched what was going on during the Gilded Age in my country, in Puerto Rico,” he told Vogue ahead of the event.

Working with Burberry’s Riccardo Tisci, the pair “mixed the men’s looks with women’s” from that time to come up with a custom ensemble featuring a tan puff-sleeve trench coat and skirt over classic blue collared shirt and black tie.
Bad Bunny topped off the look by wearing his hair in an updo and using a pair of vintage hand-held eyeglasses.

In 2023, the multi-Grammy winner dazzled in an elegant and angelic white suit with a backless blazer and 26 foot flower train to celebrate the Met's  new exhibition “Karl Lagerfeld: A Line of Beauty.”

He returned to co-chair the Met Gala in 2024 in another incredible ensemble. For that year's celebration, he wore a custom look from the Maison Margiela Artisanal Collection by John Galliano featuring a black satin corset under a navy barathea wool smoking jacket, Vogue reported.
He completed the look with a reverse-swatched navy hat, dark gloves and a dark floral fabric bouquet resembling Flor de Maga, the national flower of Puerto Rico.
In 2025, Bad Bunny wore a chocolate brown Prada suit that he paired with gold crystal-adorned gloves, a woven pava hat made from palm leaves that celebrated his Puerto Rican heritage.
He also donned a diamond brooch in the shape of the Flor de Maga.

title: Beyoncé Attends Met Gala For the First Time in a Decade Alongside Daughter Blue Ivy
summary: Beyoncé is making her grand return to the Met Gala for the first time in a decade
story: Beyoncé is making her grand return to the Met Gala for the first time in a decade.
The singer, 44, who last walked the event's red carpet in 2016, is one of the gala's co-chairs this year alongside Nicole Kidman, Venus Williams and Anna Wintour.
Follow for live updates
Beyoncé's stunning 2026 look reflects the year's dress code: "Fashion As Art," inspired by the Costume Institute's spring 2026 exhibit, “Costume Art."
She arrived on the carpet wearing an embellished ensemble by Olivier Rousteing. Alongside her was her oldest daughter, Blue Ivy Carter, 14, who made her Met Gala debut with her mom.

“I think it’s really just experiencing this through the eyes of blue and being able to relax,” Beyoncé told La La Anthony for E! News on the carpet. “And I think for me, I’m wearing Olivier Rousteing, who is someone that’s been so loyal to me, and I’ve done so many incredible, iconic looks with him. So it’s really about representing him.”
Beyoncé's Met Gala looks have evolved over the years.
She played it safe when she made her debut at the gala in 2008 wearing a strapless pink Armani Privé gown.
She made her next appearance at the gala in 2011 in a black hip-hugging Emilio Pucci gown that featured eye-catching gold embroidery. The gown featured a cut-out at the bust and a flared tulle skirt.
In 2012, she earned raves for her striking black and purple Givenchy gown. But that headline-making look almost never happened.

The then new mom to daughter Blue Ivy, who was born in January 2012, had planned to skip the gala to continue rehearsing for her "The Mrs. Carter Show World Tour," Ty Hunter, the singer's longtime stylist, told WWD in 2017.
Hours before the gala, the singer changed her mind.
“She was, like, ‘The Met is today.’ I was, like, ‘Yeah.’ And she was, like, ‘I think I want to go,’” Hunter recalled.

“Literally within a day or a couple of hours all of that happened — and it ended up being one of her most talked-about looks,” Hunter added. “I was, like, ‘Oh my God, you want to go to what? The Met?’”

"She had just had Blue and the world hadn’t seen her," he continued. "To come back with a wow after being pregnant for so long and just showing that you can still be sexy and hot as a parent, (that’s) one of the reasons that dress got a lot of attention.”

In 2015, the Grammy winner got playful with the event’s dress code — “China: Through the Looking Glass” — by donning a sheer Givenchy Haute Couture by Riccardo Tisci gown adorned with Swarovski crystals, rhinestones and other gems.
In 2016, Beyoncé's most recent trip to the Met Gala until now, the singer paired dark eye makeup with a skin-tight peach-colored latex gown by Givenchy.

title: Kendall Jenner Is Sheer Elegance at the 2026 Met Gala
summary: Kendall Jenner looked just like a Greek goddess at the 2026 Met Gala
story: Kendall Jenner looked just like a Greek goddess at the 2026 Met Gala.
The 30-year-old model tapped Zac Posen, creative director of GAP, to create a GapStudio look that honored the gala's dress code this year, "Fashion Is Art."
Follow for live updates
Jenner's stunning gown was was inspired by "Winged Victory of Samothrace," the second-century Greek statue of the goddess of victory that greets visitors to the Louvre museum in Paris.
Posen told E! News' La La Anthony he came up with the idea for the look after twisting an old Gap T-shirt to mimic the goddess's flowing tunic.
"I like that idea that art is transcendent. It’s not owned by an institution. It’s owned by the people. It’s living," said Posen.
"I like this because it’s the goddess of Nike, the goddess of game and play and of victory.
Posen added that Jenner's look may also include a "surprise" to be revealed inside the Met.
Jenner and her sisters Kylie Jenner and Kim Kardashian have been staples of the gala for years.
Kendall Jenner has been to a dozen Met Galas since she made her debut at the event as a teenager in 2014. She wore a champagne-colored strapless gown by Topshop paired with Tabitha Simmons heels.
In 2017, she won raves for wearing a sheer gown La Perla Haute Couture Collection.
In 2018, she donned an Off White jumpsuit and attended the event with the label’s founder, Virgil Abloh.
“We worked together really heavily on creating this look for me, most of our conversations were over FaceTime,” Jenner recalled while revisiting the outfit for Vogue’s “Life in Looks.”
“You always felt comfortable and safe in his arms," she added of Abloh, who died in 2021.
The following year, she and sister Kylie Jenner arrived together wearing feathery Versace ensembles, with Kendall opting for a bright orange number.
In 2021, Kendall Jenner wore a semi-sheer Givenchy gown full of embellishments that was inspired by Audrey Hepburn’s character in "My Fair Lady."
She pulled out all the stops in for the 2024 gala by walking the red carpet in an archival Givenchy dress from Alexander McQueen’s fall 1999 couture collection.
“I made a dream list of who I would ideally want to go with, and I would have died to wear something by Alexander McQueen,” Jenner told Vogue.

title: ‘Heated Rivalry’ Stars Connor Storrie and Hudson Williams Go Shirtless and Sleeveless for Met Gala Debuts
summary: The breakout stars of "Heated Rivalry," Connor Storrie and Hudson Williams, have made their Met Gala debuts in 2026
story: The breakout stars of "Heated Rivalry," Connor Storrie and Hudson Williams, have made their Met Gala debuts in 2026.

The fashion-forward actors, who play pro hockey rivals-turned-lovers Ilya Rozanov and Shane Hollander on the HBO series, walked the carpet at the annual event, which raises funds for the Metropolitan Museum of Art's Costume Institute.
Follow live updates.
Storrie was first on the Met Gala red carpet, wearing a custom Saint Laurent look.
Shortly before his debut on the carpet, Storrie was photographed leaving The Carlyle Hotel nearby on his way to the Met.
Williams showed up shortly after in a custom Balenciaga look designed by Pierpaolo Piccioli.
Storrie and Williams have made headlines for their fashionable looks since launching a whirlwind press tour after "Heated Rivalry" premiered on HBO in November 2025.
Williams made his runway debut in January at Milan Men’s Fashion Week when he opened the Dsquared2 show, reported Vogue.
He and Storrie camped it up when they presented together at the 2026 Golden Globes.

Storrie wore a custom tailored Saint Laurent suit that he paired with Tiffany & Co. jewelry while Williams wore a custom Giorgio Armani white dinner jacket with black pants and an unbuttoned white silk shirt, reported Fashion magazine.
During Paris Fashion Week in March, Storrie attended an Yves Saint Laurent fashion show to see the brand's Fall Winter 2026 collection, according to the publication.
Storrie wore a light brown suit in Saint Laurent’s signature cut. He tucked his silky brown tie into a yellow shirt — a look right out of brand’s Spring 2026 menswear collection, Vogue reported.

title: Trump’s abrupt U-turn on plan to reopen Strait of Hormuz came after backlash from allies
summary: WASHINGTON — President Donald Trump’s abrupt reversal on his plan to help ships go through the Strait of Hormuz came after a key Gulf ally suspended the U.S. military’s ability to use its bases and airspace to carry out the operation, according to two U.S. officials.
story: WASHINGTON — President Donald Trump’s abrupt reversal on his plan to help ships go through the Strait of Hormuz came after a key Gulf ally suspended the U.S. military’s ability to use its bases and airspace to carry out the operation, according to two U.S. officials.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

Trump surprised Gulf allies by announcing “Project Freedom” on social media Sunday afternoon, the officials said, angering leadership in Saudi Arabia. In response, the Kingdom informed the U.S. it would not allow the U.S. military to fly aircraft from Prince Sultan Airbase southeast of Riyadh or fly through Saudi airspace to support the effort, the officials said.

A call between Trump and Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman did not resolve the issue, the two U.S. officials said, forcing the president to pause Project Freedom in order to restore U.S. military access to the critical airspace.
Other close Gulf allies were also caught off guard; the president spoke with leaders in Qatar after the effort had already begun.

A Saudi source told NBC News that Trump and the crown prince “have been in touch regularly.” Saudi officials were also in touch with Trump, Vice President JD Vance, U.S. Central Command and Secretary of State Marco Rubio, the source added.

Asked whether the announcement of Project Freedom caught the Saudi leaders by surprise, the Saudi source said: “The problem with that premise is that things are happening quickly in real time.” The source said Saudi Arabia was “very supportive of the diplomatic efforts” by Pakistan to broker a deal between Iran and the U.S. to end the war.

A White House official said in a statement when asked about some Gulf state leaders being caught off guard by the announcement of the U.S. effort to help ships transit the Strait of Hormuz, “Regional allies were notified in advance.”

A Middle Eastern diplomat said the U.S. did not coordinate Project Freedom with the Omanis until after Trump made the announcement. “The U.S. made an announcement and then coordinated with us,” the diplomat said, adding, “we were not upset or angry.”

Trump had announced the operation over the weekend as a way to break Iran’s blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, and his top national security leaders spent much of Tuesday talking up the effort in public briefings at the Pentagon and White House, only to have the president suddenly halt the operation roughly 36 hours after it began.

The U.S. military had been lining up a number of additional ships in the Gulf for transit through the strait when the operation was stopped, a U.S. official said. U.S. Central Command had earlier announced that two U.S. flagged ships had made it through the strait as part of Project Freedom.
In his post, Trump said Project Freedom would be “paused for a short period of time to see whether or not” an agreement to resolve the war “can be finalized and signed.”

The U.S. military maintains fighter aircraft, refueling tankers and air defenses at Prince Sultan Airbase in Saudi Arabia. The Saudis allowed the U.S. to fly aircraft from there to support the war in Iran, as well as allowing aircraft based in nearby countries to fly over.

“Because of geography, you need cooperation from regional partners to utilize their airspace along their borders,” one U.S. official said. In some cases there is no other way around, the official explained. Military aircraft were critical to protect ships during Project Freedom, essentially providing a defensive umbrella.

The U.S. military refers to permission to use another country’s territory as ABO, which stands for access, basing and overflight. Fighter jets, refueling tankers and support aircraft all need permission to fly from key regional allies. Saudi Arabia and Jordan are critical for allowing aircraft to base there, Kuwait is critical for overflight, and Oman for both overflight and naval logistics.

Trump called the emir of Qatar after Project Freedom began, and a Qatari official said in a statement that they discussed the ceasefire agreement and “implications for maritime security and global supply chains.” The statement said the emir emphasized the importance of de-escalation.

The U.S. military continues to maintain a presence in and around the Gulf. It has a bigger footprint in the region than it did on Feb. 28 when the war began. There are two carrier strike groups in the region, and the Pentagon has brought in additional logistics and support and has re-supplied stockpiles.

Project Freedom briefly provided U.S. military surveillance, firepower and personnel on board ships so they could safely transit out of the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz, as Iranian forces continued to threaten and attack ships in transit through the critical waterway. Pentagon officials said the operation was separate from the bombing campaign that began on Feb. 28 and was dubbed “Epic Fury.”

The Trump administration has attempted to push forward on a negotiated agreement to end the hostilities. Iran was reviewing another peace proposal with the U.S., Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei said in an interview with Iran’s semiofficial ISNA news agency. He said once the regime has assessed it, Iran will discuss it with Pakistan, which has been acting as a mediator. Axios first reported details of the proposal.
In a social media post early Wednesday, Trump didn’t detail the proposal, but said the war could end if “Iran agrees to give what has been agreed to.”
“They want to make a deal,” Trump said from the Oval Office on Wednesday. He said there had been “very good talks over the last 24 hours.”

The political pressure is mounting on Trump ahead of the November midterm elections, when Republicans will be fighting to preserve their narrow margin in the House and majority in the Senate. In an interview with PBS on Wednesday, the president said it’s possible U.S. negotiators could strike a deal with Iran’s regime before he travels to Beijing next week for a meeting with his Chinese counterpart, Xi Jinping.
“I think it’s got a very good chance of ending, and if it doesn’t end, we have to go back to bombing the hell out of them,” Trump told PBS.

Several close Trump confidants have encouraged the president to “finish the job” in Iran by taking out the rest of the regime’s conventional military assets, even insisting that the offensive could be completed by the time of the critical China trip, according to multiple former U.S. officials.
China’s Foreign Minister Wang Yi met with Iranian leaders Wednesday and said it was critical the war ended as soon as possible.

“We believe that a comprehensive ceasefire is urgently needed, that a resumption of hostilities is not acceptable, and that it is particularly important to remain committed to dialogue and negotiations,” Wang said in a video of the meeting accessed by The Associated Press.
In a post on social media, a senior Iranian parliament official called the latest proposal a “wish list until it becomes a reality.”

“Americans will not obtain through a failed war what they failed to gain in face-to-face negotiations, Ebrahim Rezaei said on X. “Iran has its finger on the trigger and is ready; if they do not surrender and grant the necessary concessions, or if they or their devilish henchdog allies try to act mischievously, we will deliver a harsh and regret-inducing response.”
But a Jordanian official told NBC News that the diplomatic efforts were serious.
“The Iranians don’t have the economic means to keep this going,” the official said. “Their economy is failing, they can’t pay salaries.”

title: Map: See how gas prices have changed in the county where you live
summary: How much gas prices have increased during the Iran war depends entirely on where you live, an NBC News analysis of average gas price data from AAA shows.
story: Gas prices are up across the country, but nowhere are they up as much as in northern Kentucky’s Kenton County, where an average gallon of gas would run you $4.92 a gallon Tuesday, nearly double the price it was at the start of the Iran war.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

How much gas prices have increased depends entirely on where you live, an NBC News analysis of average gas price data from AAA shows. Kenton County prices were up $2.32 a gallon, while Fisher County in Texas, population 3,692, prices had only increased 60 cents as of Tuesday.

It also depends on the state. In Georgia, gas prices are up an average of $1.16 per gallon, the lowest state-level increase in the nation. But in Ohio, the average price is up $2.06 a gallon, the largest state-level increase in the U.S.
This map, showing the latest gas prices and the change in prices since the start of the war, will be updated daily.

title: Price of oil plunges and markets surge on report U.S. and Iran are near deal
summary: The price of oil plunged and stocks surged after a new report said the U.S. believed it was nearing a deal to end the war with Iran.
story: The price of oil plunged and stocks surged Wednesday after a report said the U.S. believed it was nearing a deal to end the war with Iran and eventually reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
The S&P 500 closed higher by 1.5% and the Nasdaq Composite jumped 2%, with both indexes closing at record highs.
The Russell 2000 index also added 1.5%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average traded up by more than 610 points.
U.S. crude oil plunged by as much as 15%, to $88 per barrel, and international Brent crude oil fell as much as 11%, to $96 per barrel.

The moves moderated slightly after Trump told the New York Post in an interview that it was “too soon” to prepare to sign a peace deal. Still, U.S. crude oil closed down by 7%, at $95.08 per barrel, and Brent closed down 7.8%, at $101.27 per barrel.
Wholesale gas prices also dropped by 5%, and heating oil, a proxy for jet fuel, fell 6%. Even with Wednesday’s drops, the price of oil is still up more than 65% since the start of the year.

An end to hostilities is far from assured. Officials in Washington and Tehran were working on "a one-page memorandum of understanding to end the war and set a framework for more detailed nuclear negotiations," Axios reported Wednesday morning, citing two U.S. officials and two additional sources.

A spokesperson for Iran's Foreign Affairs Ministry told the Iranian news outlet ISNA that a U.S. proposal was under review and that Iran planned to convey its assessment of it to Pakistan, which is mediating.
Meanwhile, Iran's navy said Wednesday morning on X that “with aggressor’s threats neutralized & new protocols in place, safe, stable passage through SOH will be ensured.”

President Donald Trump, in his own post on Truth Social minutes later, said the war would end and the strait would reopen "assuming Iran agrees to give what has been agreed to, which is perhaps a big assumption." He warned Tehran to agree to his terms or "the bombing starts, and it will be, sadly, at a much higher level and intensity than it was before."
The sharp move higher in stock indexes was also supported by double-digit gains in some of the largest tech companies following a wave of positive earnings reports.
European markets also jumped, with the broad Stoxx 600 index closing higher by 2.3%.
Bond yields also dropped sharply, with the 10-year and 30-year U.S. government bond yields dropping near their lowest levels since last week.
Falling bond yields can quickly translate into relief for consumers.

As bond rates have risen during the war, so have borrowing costs for consumers taking out loans or mortgages. As of Monday afternoon, the average 30-year fixed-rate mortgage rate was 6.44%, down sharply from Sunday and its lowest level since Friday, according to Mortgage News Daily data. The rate could fall even further in the days ahead if Wednesday's market moves continue.
Shortly before the Axios report and the market reaction, the average U.S. retail gas price jumped past $4.50 per gallon for the first time since July 2022.

title: Boy, 14, shot dead by Israeli settlers in West Bank amid escalation in violence
summary: AL-MUGHAYYIR, OCCUPIED WEST BANK – A boy’s blood still stained the sidewalk in front of his school, days after an Israeli settler shot him dead
story: AL-MUGHAYYIR, OCCUPIED WEST BANK – A boy’s blood still stained the sidewalk in front of his school, days after an Israeli settler shot him dead.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
Aws al-Nasaan, 14, was gunned down in broad daylight last week in the small Palestinian village of Al-Mughayyir, in the occupied West Bank.

His death comes amid a dramatic escalation in settler violence, alongside a push to expand Israel's control over the West Bank through new laws, settlement expansions and security crackdowns limiting Palestinians' freedom. According to data from the United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs, Israeli settlers and forces have killed at least 42 Palestinians so far this year.

The attack, caught on video from multiple angles, took place just after noon on Tuesday, April 21. Video obtained by NBC News shows a bearded man in an Israeli military uniform firing down the hill toward the school. He crouches to get a better angle and keeps shooting.
Video shot from the road to the east side of the school shows bullets hitting the wall. In one of the videos, al-Nasaan’s body can be seen on the ground as his friends shout for help.

“They ran out of the gates trying to escape,” the school’s principal Bassem Abu Assaf said, pointing out the bullet holes still visible in the stone wall last week. “The shooting was crazy, just crazy, nonstop shooting.”
Construction worker Jihad Abu Naim, 32, was also killed in the attack, the principal said.

“This is supposed to be a safe place,” said Abu Assaf, who is responsible for 460 students. “School is a right for every child,” he added. But, fearful of another attack, classes were cancelled for a week.

Palestinian eyewitnesses say Israeli soldiers were on the scene within minutes and did nothing to stop or apprehend the shooter. Instead, they lobbed tear gas into the crowd of students and villagers outside of the school, Abu Assaf and multiple eyewitnesses said.
Paramedics killed in Israeli strikes in Lebanon

In response to questions from NBC News about the Al-Mughayyir attack, the Israeli military confirmed the shooter was a reservist soldier and said an investigation has been launched. A statement said: “The IDF reservist has been suspended from reserve duty pending the conclusion of the investigation and his weapon has been confiscated.”
According to rights groups, Israeli settler violence has surged in the occupied West Bank in recent weeks.

Israeli human rights group Yesh Din documented nearly 400 incidents in the first 40 days of the Iran war. Incidents, many caught on video, include shootings, beatings, arson, land destruction, livestock theft and intimidation.
“The root reason is that everyone is looking elsewhere,” the organization’s CEO, Ziv Stahl said of the record spike in violence. “The world is distracted.”

title: Anger and despair among Jewish Britons after spate of attacks
summary: LONDON — Britain in 2026 is a place where Jack Hur doesn’t feel safe wearing his Star of David pendant.
story: LONDON — Britain in 2026 is a place where Jack Hur doesn’t feel safe wearing his Star of David pendant. It’s where some Jewish mothers tell their teenage sons to remove their kippot before boarding the tube. It’s where dinner-table talk of leaving the country no longer seems outlandish.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
Standing behind the counter of Sulam’s Kosher Food Store, Hur, 32, produces his Star of David necklace from underneath his sweatshirt. From his back pocket he reveals a kippah.
“I only wear this sometimes,” he says, unfolding the small, black skullcap. “It depends where I am.”
Ultimately for Jews, he said, “Britain just isn’t safe.”

Golders Green, a hub for Britain’s tiny Jewish community, feels like a neighborhood under siege. Antisemitism had been surging in the United Kingdom since the Hamas-led Oct. 7, 2023, terror attack on Israel and Israel’s subsequent offensive in the Gaza Strip.

There were 3,700 reported antisemitic incidents last year, more than double the number in 2022, according to the Community Security Trust, a charity that coordinates security measures at Jewish institutions and tracks this data.
But a spate of attacks against synagogues and other Jewish sites in recent weeks has brought that to another level.

This week, two people were stabbed in Golders Green. Police have arrested and charged with attempted murder a Somali-born man who in 2020 had been referred to the government’s early prevention counterterrorism program, a case that was closed later that year. Essa Suleiman, 45, appeared in court Friday but did not enter a plea.

The Metropolitan Police classified it as a terrorist  attack, and authorities raised the national threat level from “substantial” —  indicating an attack is likely — to “severe,” meaning an “attack is highly likely in the next six months.”

“The U.K. has been experiencing a gradual increase in terrorist threats for some time, driven by a rise in both Islamist and extreme right-wing terrorism,” said Laurence Taylor, assistant commissioner and head of counterterrorism at the Metropolitan Police.

“Our casework is increasing across a number of ideologies and within that we are seeing an elevated threat to Jewish and Israeli individuals and institutions in the UK,” he said in a statement Thursday.
Britain’s chief rabbi, Ephraim Mirvis, agrees with Hur.
“If you are visibly Jewish, you’re not safe,” he told the BBC this week.

Some of these incidents have been claimed by a new group called Harakat Ashab al-Yamin al-Islamia, or HAYI, which analysts say has links to Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, or IRGC. It’s unclear the extent to which HAYI had any involvement in the planning or preparation, or if it is merely piggybacking onto the assaults to gain publicity.

Either way, this is jolting for many of Britain’s 290,000 Jews, many of whom prided themselves in their unfussy integration into British society. For years, members of the community have played prominent roles, with Jewish-born politician Benjamin Disraeli, one of the country's best-known prime ministers, taking office in 1868. In the 1930s, more than 10,000 Jewish children gained refuge from Nazi tyranny as part of the “Kindertransport” program.

Antisemitism has deep-seated historical roots here too, with some harmful myths stretching back as far as King Edward I's expulsion of the Jews from England in 1290 - an edict that remained in place for more than 350 years.
On a warm morning Friday on Golders Green Road, near where the stabbings happened, the mood was less of fear, more anger.
There was a steady cadence of police cars humming past, as well as officers stationed outside the local London Underground station. For many locals, this was too late.

“Since we’ve been standing here, we’ve had three police vehicles gone by,” said Alvin Ormonde, 75, a local planning consultant whom friends describe as an influential community figure. This was an unusual sight, he said.
He was chatting with friends beneath a tangled Union Flag, one of several hanging from a lamppost on this wide street flanked by stores interspersed with large, postwar houses.
“In a week’s time, the police presence will be back to where it was,” said another man, who like many here is distrustful of what he sees as a biased media and declined to give his name.
Much of the anger is directed at Prime Minister Keir Starmer, who, when visiting Golders Green this week, was heckled by a crowd who chanted “Keir Starmer, Jew-harmer” at him.

In response to that outpouring of anger, the government said Starmer acknowledged fear within the community. The prime minister then issued some of his strongest remarks on the subject yet, calling out the “extreme racism” that had “left a minority community in this country scared, intimidated, wondering if they belong.”
Opponents’ main source of anger is what they see as Starmer’s lax approach to the near weekly pro-Palestinian marches in London and elsewhere that they characterize as “hate marches.”

Some protesters have displayed antisemitic sentiment, and used language that conflates enmity toward Israel, Zionism and Jews themselves. The organizers of the demonstrations deny this accusation, saying their target is merely the Israeli government rather than Jews in Britain or anywhere else.
Among NBC News’ conversations in Golders Green, there was an unavoidable thread of animosity toward Muslims in general.

“They want to take over the whole place and chuck everyone out, including the British,” said one man in a dark suit and wearing payot and a kippah on Golders Green Road. Citing a mix of media mistrust and community anxiety, he too declined to give his name.

“We are all refugees,” said Ezra Kahn, 88, after ambling up and joining the conversation. “But there is a big difference between the refugees we were and the refugees that are coming in by the boatload all the time. We get on with our job, we educate our children to be good citizens and we contribute to taxes,” he said. “The refugees who are now coming by the boatload, they’re coming here and immediately start making trouble.”
It must be said that many Jewish people, including their mainstream leaders, would disavow this, insisting instead on interfaith tolerance and respect.

The Board of Deputies of British Jews, a leading community organization, says on its website that “we have a long track record of standing with Muslim communities in the fight against anti-Muslim hatred.”
And that’s a sentiment echoed by many people in Golders Green too.
Shopkeepers say the roads are quiet for a sunny Friday hours before the Jewish Sabbath.

It’s not just the stabbing, the arson attacks against synagogues, the firebombing of four Jewish ambulances two months ago; it’s that antisemitic language feels closer to the surface, less hidden in the politeness of British life, than it ever did previously.

Nigel Farage, the leader of the hard-right populist Reform UK party and a friend of President Donald Trump, has faced his own allegations. More than 30 of his former classmates have accused him of regular antisemitism when he was a schoolboy more than 40 years ago, including singing songs about gassing Jewish people. Farage has denied the allegations.
Meanwhile on the opposite end of the spectrum, two Green Party candidates have been arrested ahead of local elections next week, accused of posting antisemitic social media posts.
An idle scroll through any social media feed will reveal violent language against Jewish people that was considered widely unutterable a few years ago.
The upshot is not only that Jewish Britons feel unsafe. A poll by the Campaign Against Antisemitism last year found that 61% of the community said they had considered leaving Britain altogether.

“You cannot be Jewish in Britain today,” said one woman in a long, white summer dress and designer sunglasses, also declining to give her name. “My friends who have teenage sons tell them to take off their kippot before they get on the tube so they are not attacked.”

Outside the Beth Shmuel Synagogue, two men sit guarding the premises, both wearing non-religious dress except their kippot, smoking cigarettes and drinking coffee. This level of security is a  depressing necessity in modern-day Britain.

“You know, Jewish people will be fine,” one of them said. “We may have to move to a different country, we may get to eat a little less sushi, but it’s nothing we haven’t done before.” He added, “It’s the regular Brits that will suffer in the long term with what’s happening to the country.”

title: Oil rain and toxic fumes: Black Sea town faces environmental catastrophe as Ukrainian strikes rock Russia
summary: The Black Sea isn’t meant to be this black
story: The Black Sea isn’t meant to be this black.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
A popular seaside town in Russia has seen oil wash onto its beaches, dark drops of rain and oil soot land on homes, and plumes of thick acrid smoke billow above its red rooftops.

Residents of the usually picturesque Russian resort town of Tuapse were this week warned not to leave the homes or open windows as authorities tackled intense fires from a series of Ukrainian drone attacks on the town’s oil refinery.
The huge blaze was finally extinguished Thursday, the local governor said, though another drone attack hit the town’s sea terminal Friday, sparking a fire.

Tuapse now faces a daunting cleanup operation and a hit to its upcoming tourist season. Its residents will also be awaiting further attacks, as Ukraine intensifies its campaign to disrupt the Russian oil industry and deprive the Kremlin of crucial funding for its war machine.
“You can smell it anywhere in the town,” one resident, Svetlana, told NBC News of the acrid stench that gets stronger the closer you get to Tuapse’s center.

Svetlana, who did not want her last name shared out of fear of repercussions for talking about a sensitive security topic, said she could see smoke from her window even though she lives on the edge of town, far from the refinery.

title: U.S. monitoring hantavirus cruise passengers; dozens left ship after 1st death
summary: The United States was on Thursday among the countries urgently tracking dozens of passengers who traveled on the cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak
story: The United States was on Thursday among the countries urgently tracking dozens of passengers who traveled on the cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

The luxury cruise has fueled a growing international infectious disease response, after the deaths of three people and suspected infections of at least five more. A possible new case also emerged Thursday, apparently involving a woman who had not been a passenger on the ship.

Weeks after the first death on board, 29 living passengers left the ship without contact tracing in St. Helena, a tiny and remote island in the middle of the Atlantic Ocean, cruise operator Oceanwide Expeditions said in a statement Thursday. The body of a Dutch man who died at sea also left the boat at this stage.
The company said it was “working to establish” the whereabouts of all those who disembarked on April 24 and had contacted each of them. It said the number included six Americans.

One person who was on board the Hondius is at home in Arizona, two are in Georgia and an unknown number are back in California, according to authorities in those states — none were said to be showing symptoms of the rare virus.
The passengers who left the boat include 11 other nationalities but the parent country of two people is unknown.
It also emerged Thursday that a flight attendant was being tested for hantavirus at a hospital in the Netherlands.

“I can confirm that a stewardess is in hospital now and she is being tested for the virus,” a spokesperson for the Dutch Health Ministry told NBC News. The department did not say whether she was ill or showing symptoms of the virus, which is rare but potentially deadly.

KLM Royal Dutch Airlines said Wednesday that a Dutch woman who died after contracting hantavirus was “briefly” on board a flight from Johannesburg to Amsterdam and was removed from the plane before takeoff.
It is not clear if the flight attendant was on this same flight. KLM said in a statement it does not comment on individual cases “for privacy reasons.”

The World Health Organization and national health officials have repeatedly stressed that the risk to the public is low and person-to-person transmission was only possible through close personal contact, such as between a couple.

Hantavirus is typically contracted through contact with rodents. The WHO however confirmed this week that the outbreak is the Andes strain of the virus which, unlike other strains, is known to be transmissible between people.
Health experts have said that even this strain is not as easily passed on as airborne diseases such as influenza or Covid-19.

A travel vlogger on board the Hondius told NBC News that passengers were “not well informed” about the situation on board. He recorded video of the captain telling passengers that the ship was “not infectious” after announcing the first death.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google

Almost 150 people are still on board the Hondius under isolation protocols as the ship makes its way north from Cape Verde in western Africa to the Spanish-controlled Canary Islands. The journey is expected to last three to four days, though there is reluctance from the leader of the islands’ regional government to accept the ship.

Travel blogger Jake Rosmarin, who is on the Hondius, said on his Tiktok account: “Other than the two passengers already referenced publicly and now evacuated, everyone else onboard is doing well and remains in good spirits.”
Spain’s interior ministry said that the ship was expected to arrive there Sunday and evacuations would begin Monday, “if all goes well.”

On Monday, Oceanwide said there were 17 Americans on board. The CDC said in a statement Wednesday night that both it and the State Department were closely monitoring the  status of Americans who had been on the ship.

“The Department of State is leading a coordinated, whole-of-government response including direct contact with passengers, diplomatic coordination, and engagement with domestic and international health authorities,” the statement said.
The CDC added that the “risk to the American public is extremely low.”

The three people to have died so far are a Dutch couple and a German national, while a British man is being treated in hospital in South Africa. It emerged Wednesday that a man who had left the ship was being treated in Zurich with suspected hantavirus.

Three patients were transported from the Hondius on Wednesday for medical treatment in the Netherlands and Germany, as health officials switch their focus to finding and monitoring the dozens of people who left the ship along its voyage.

Hantavirus infection in humans is extremely rare and has never been previously recorded on a cruise ship. The CDC monitors the disease and its data show that there were 890 confirmed cases between 1993 and 2023.

Argentinian authorities said that a program of rodent trapping would take place in the city of Ushuaia, where the Hondius began its journey, and that it would carry out 2,500 diagnostic tests to try to find the origin of the outbreak.

title: Marco Rubio meets with Pope Leo after Trump’s criticism over Iran war
summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio discussed the Middle East with Pope Leo at the Vatican on Thursday, at an audience that follows President Donald Trump’s criticism of the first American pontiff over the Iran war.
story: Secretary of State Marco Rubio discussed the Middle East with Pope Leo at the Vatican on Thursday, at an audience that follows President Donald Trump’s criticism of the first American pontiff over the Iran war.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
The closed-door meeting was the first between the head of the Catholic Church and a Trump cabinet official in nearly a year.
It comes at a fraught time between Washington and the Holy See, with relations at a low after weeks of presidential attacks on Leo and the Chicago-born pope’s outspoken responses.
State Department spokesperson Tommy Pigott said they discussed “the situation in the Middle East and topics of mutual interest in the Western Hemisphere.”
He said in a statement that the meeting “underscored the strong relationship” between the U.S. and the Vatican, “and their shared commitment to promoting peace and human dignity.”
Rubio was inside the Apostolic Palace, the Pope’s official residence, for more than two hours.
